member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Internals.TypedBinding.ForSingleNestingLevel``2(System.String,System.Func{``0,``1},System.Action{``0,``1},Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingMode,Microsoft.Maui.Controls.IValueConverter,System.Object,System.Object)

summary.para: This factory method was added to simplify creating typed bindings for a property that isn't nested which is the most common scenario.

This factory method must be used carefully. As the name implies, it is only applicable when the getter and setter access a property directly on the source object. Whenever the property is nested two or more levels deep, create the binding manually and construct the handlers array for that usecase.

member.typeparam: The type of the source object.
The type of the property.
member.param: The name of the property.
The getter function to retrieve the property value from the source object.
The optional setter action to set the property value on the source object.
The binding mode.
The value converter.
The converter parameter.
The source object.
member.returns: The typed binding.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindableObject
member.summary: Provides a mechanism to propagate data changes from one object to another. Enables validation, type coercion, and an event system.
member.remarks: The class provides a data storage mechanism that enables the application developer to synchronize data between objects in response to changes, for example, between the View and View Model in the MVVM design pattern. All of the visual elements in the Microsoft.Maui.Controls namespace inherit from class, so they can all be used to bind the data behind their user interface.

remarks.para: Attached properties are bindable properties that are bound to an object other than their parent. Often, they are used for child items in tables and grids, where data about the location of an item is maintained by its parent, but must be accessed from the child item itself.

When using the callback, note that if multiple attached instances share the same , the callback cannot determine which specific property triggered the change. Consider using separate callback methods for properties that require different handling.

member.summary: Gets or sets the mode for this binding.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingBase.StringFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the string format applied to the bound value.
member.value: A standard composite format string. For single-value bindings use one placeholder (e.g., {0:C2}).
member.remarks: Used to format or composite the resulting bound value for display. Implementations follow standard semantics.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingBase.TargetNullValue
member.summary: Gets or sets the value to use when the binding successfully resolves the source path and the resulting source value is .
member.value: The value that will replace a resolved source value when updating the target.

remarks.para: TargetNullValue acts like a null-coalescing value for the binding source result: if the binding path resolves and the value is , the target receives TargetNullValue instead. It is not used when the binding cannot resolve (e.g. missing property, conversion error) — in those cases is considered.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingBase.FallbackValue
member.summary: Gets or sets the value used when the binding cannot produce a source value (e.g. path not found, conversion failure).
member.value: The fallback value used instead of the target property's default value when source resolution fails entirely.

remarks.para: FallbackValue is applied when the binding engine fails to obtain a value (e.g., missing source, unresolved path, or type conversion failure within the binding engine itself). It is not used for errors that occur inside value converters; such errors may be handled by the converter or use different fallback mechanisms. If the source resolves to , is applied if set.
Together with this allows differentiating between a legitimate value and an unresolved binding.

Delegate invoked by the framework to perform collection access under synchronization.
member.remarks: The framework holds only a weak reference to the collection. The callback receives parameters indicating whether write access is required; implementers should perform appropriate locking (often on ) before invoking the supplied access delegate.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingMode
member.summary: The direction of changes propagation for bindings.

remarks.para: The following examples shows some BindingMode use cases.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingMode.Default
member.summary: When used in Bindings, indicates that the Binding should use the . When used in BindableProperty declaration, defaults to BindingMode.OneWay.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingMode.TwoWay
member.summary: Indicates that the binding should propagates changes from source (usually the View Model) to target (the BindableObject) in both directions.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingMode.OneWay
member.summary: Indicates that the binding should only propagate changes from source (usually the View Model) to target (the BindableObject). This is the default mode for most BindableProperty values.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingMode.OneWayToSource
member.summary: Indicates that the binding should only propagate changes from target (the BindableObject) to source (usually the View Model). This is mainly used for read-only BindableProperty values.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingMode.OneTime
member.summary: Indicates that the binding will be applied only when the binding context changes and the value will not be monitored for changes with INotifyPropertyChanged.

member.summary: Gets or sets the padding for the button. This is a bindable property.

member.summary: The backing store for the bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.BorderColor
member.summary: Gets or sets a color that describes the border stroke color of the button. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: This property has no effect if is set to 0. On Android this property will not have an effect unless is set to a non-default color.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.CornerRadius
member.summary: Gets or sets the corner radius for the button, in device-independent units. This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.BorderWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the width of the border, in device-independent units. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: Set this value to a non-zero value in order to have a visible border.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.ContentLayout
member.summary: Gets or sets an object that controls the position of the button image and the spacing between the button's image and the button's text. This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.Command
member.summary: Gets or sets the command to invoke when the button is activated. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: This property is used to associate a command with an instance of a button. This property is most often set in the MVVM pattern to bind callbacks back into the ViewModel. is controlled by the if set.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.CommandParameter
member.summary: Gets or sets the parameter to pass to the property. The default value is . This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.ImageSource
member.summary: Allows you to display a bitmap image on the Button. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: For more options have a look at .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.Text
member.summary: Gets or sets the text displayed as the content of the button. The default value is . This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: Changing the text of a button will trigger a layout cycle.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.TextColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the for the text of the button. This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.CharacterSpacing
member.summary: Gets or sets the spacing between each of the characters of when displayed on the button. This is a bindable property.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.SendClicked
member.summary: Internal method to trigger the event. Should not be called manually outside of .NET MAUI.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.IsPressed
member.summary: Gets whether or not the button is currently pressed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.SendPressed
member.summary: Internal method to trigger the event. Should not be called manually outside of .NET MAUI.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.SendReleased
member.summary: Internal method to trigger the event. Should not be called manually outside of .NET MAUI.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.FontAttributes
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the font for the text of this button is bold, italic, or neither. This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.FontFamily
member.summary: Gets or sets the font family for the text of this entry. This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.FontSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the font for the text of this entry. This is a bindable property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.FontAutoScalingEnabled
member.summary: Determines whether or not the font of this entry should scale automatically according to the operating system settings. Default value is . This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: Typically this should always be enabled for accessibility reasons.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button.TextTransform
member.summary: Applies text transformation to the displayed on this button. This is a bindable property.

member.summary: Forces a layout cycle on the element and all of its descendants.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Compatibility.Layout.LayoutChildIntoBoundingRegion(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement,Microsoft.Maui.Graphics.Rect)
member.summary: Positions a child element into a bounding region while respecting the child elements and .
member.param: The child element to be positioned.
The bounding region in which the child should be positioned.
member.remarks: This method is called in the layout cycle after the general regions for each child have been calculated. This method will handle positioning the element relative to the bounding region given if the bounding region given is larger than the child's desired size.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Compatibility.Layout.LowerChild(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.View)
member.summary: Sends a child to the back of the visual stack.
member.param: The view to lower in the visual stack.
member.remarks: Children are internally stored in visual stack order. This means that raising or lowering a child also changes the order in which the children are enumerated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Compatibility.Layout.RaiseChild(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.View)
member.summary: Sends a child to the front of the visual stack.
member.param: The view to raise in the visual stack.
member.remarks: Children are internally stored in visual stack order. This means that raising or lowering a child also changes the order in which the children are enumerated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Compatibility.Layout.InvalidateLayout
member.summary: Invalidates the current layout.
member.remarks: Calling this method will invalidate the measure and triggers a new layout cycle.

member.summary: Positions and sizes the children of a layout.

A value representing the y coordinate of the child region bounding box.
A value representing the width of the child region bounding box.
A value representing the height of the child region bounding box.
member.remarks: Implementors wishing to change the default behavior of a Layout should override this method. It is suggested to still call the base method and modify its calculated results.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ContentPropertyAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the property of the type that is the (default) content property.

remarks.para: XAML processor uses to determine the content property.
Decorating types with ContentPropertyAttribute allows shorter XAML syntax. As
remarks.example:
has a ContentProperty attribute applied, this XAML is valid:
<ContentView> <Label Text="Hello, Forms"/> </ContentView>
This is equivalent to the following, more explicit XAML
<ContentView> <ContentView.Content> <Label Text="Hello, Forms"/> </ContentView.Content> </ContentView>

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DisplayRotationStateTrigger
member.summary: Trigger that activates when the device display rotation matches the specified .

remarks.para: The enables developers to create visual states that are triggered based on the device's display rotation. Unlike which only differentiates between portrait and landscape orientations, this trigger provides granular control over specific rotation angles (0°, 90°, 180°, 270°).

This trigger is particularly useful for applications that need to respond to specific device orientations, such as games that have different layouts for each rotation state or apps that need to handle upside-down orientations differently.

example.para: The following example shows how to use to change the background color based on device rotation:
example.code: <ContentPage.Resources> <Style TargetType="ContentPage"> <Setter Property="VisualStateManager.VisualStateGroups"> <VisualStateGroupList> <VisualStateGroup> <VisualState Name="Rotation0"> <VisualState.StateTriggers> <controls:DisplayRotationStateTrigger Rotation="Rotation0" /> </VisualState.StateTriggers> <VisualState.Setters> <Setter Property="BackgroundColor" Value="Red" /> </VisualState.Setters> </VisualState> <VisualState Name="Rotation90"> <VisualState.StateTriggers> <controls:DisplayRotationStateTrigger Rotation="Rotation90" /> </VisualState.StateTriggers> <VisualState.Setters> <Setter Property="BackgroundColor" Value="Green" /> </VisualState.Setters> </VisualState> <VisualState Name="Rotation180"> <VisualState.StateTriggers> <controls:DisplayRotationStateTrigger Rotation="Rotation180" /> </VisualState.StateTriggers> <VisualState.Setters> <Setter Property="BackgroundColor" Value="Blue" /> </VisualState.Setters> </VisualState> <VisualState Name="Rotation270"> <VisualState.StateTriggers> <controls:DisplayRotationStateTrigger Rotation="Rotation270" /> </VisualState.StateTriggers> <VisualState.Setters> <Setter Property="BackgroundColor" Value="Yellow" /> </VisualState.Setters> </VisualState> </VisualStateGroup> </VisualStateGroupList> </Setter> </Style> </ContentPage.Resources>

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DisplayRotationStateTrigger.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.remarks: The trigger will immediately evaluate its state based on the current device display rotation.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DisplayRotationStateTrigger.Rotation
member.summary: Gets or sets the display rotation that will activate this trigger.
member.value: The value that will activate this trigger when the device display matches it.

remarks.para: The trigger will be active when the device's current display rotation matches this property value. Possible values are:

item.description: - Device is in its natural position (0 degrees)

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Element
member.summary: Provides the base class for all Microsoft.Maui.Controls hierarchal elements. This class contains all the methods and properties required to represent an element in the Microsoft.Maui.Controls hierarchy.

remarks.para: Important categories of visual elements are noted in the following table:

item.description: An that occupies an area on the screen, has a visual appearance, and can obtain touch input.
Cells are elements meant to be added to or .
A that occupies most or all of the screen and contains a single child.
have a single child of type , while subclasses of have a collection of multiple children views, including other layouts.
The lower part of the diagram shows the Microsoft.Maui.Controls classes for universally-available controls, such as and .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Element.FindByName(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the element that has the specified name.
member.param: The name of the element to be found.
member.returns: The element that has the specified name, or if no element with the specified name is found.
member.exception: Thrown if the element's namescope couldn't be found.

remarks.para: This method searches for named elements within the current namescope. The search scope is determined by traversing up the visual tree from the current element until a namescope is found. Typically, each page, content view, or data template defines its own namescope.
The search is limited to elements that have been registered in the same namescope, which includes:

item.description: Elements with x:Name attributes defined in XAML within the same namescope
Child elements and their descendants within the same namescope boundary

remarks.para: Elements in different namescopes (such as different pages or data templates) are not accessible from each other through this method.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.GradientStop
member.summary: Represents a color and position within a gradient.
member.remarks: A is used to specify colors at specific positions in a or . The gradient is calculated by interpolating between the colors at their specified offsets.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.GradientStop.ColorProperty
member.summary: Bindable property for .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.GradientStop.Color
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of the gradient stop. This is a bindable property.
member.value: A that represents the color at this gradient stop.
member.remarks: This property defines the color that will be displayed at the position specified by the property in the gradient.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.GradientStop.OffsetProperty
member.summary: Bindable property for .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.GradientStop.Offset
member.summary: Gets or sets the position of the gradient stop within the gradient. This is a bindable property.
member.value: A value between 0.0 and 1.0 that represents the relative position of the gradient stop. The default value is 0.0.

remarks.para: The property specifies where the associated appears in the gradient. A value of 0.0 represents the start of the gradient, while 1.0 represents the end. Values between 0.0 and 1.0 position the color proportionally along the gradient path.
For a , the gradient path runs from the to the . For a , the gradient radiates from the center to the outer edge defined by the .

Multiple gradient stops at different offsets create smooth color transitions. Gradient stops do not need to be added in order of their offset values, as they will be automatically sorted during rendering.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.remarks: Creates a gradient stop with default values.

member.param: The color of the gradient stop.
The position of the gradient stop within the gradient, ranging from 0.0 (start) to 1.0 (end).
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified color and offset.
member.remarks: The gradient stop can be added to a to define color transitions in a gradient brush.

member.name: E:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.HybridWebView.WebResourceRequested
member.summary:
Raised when a web resource is requested. This event allows the application to intercept the request and provide a custom response. The event handler can set the property to true to indicate that the request has been handled and no further processing is needed. If the event handler does set this property to true, it must also call the
or method to provide a response to the request.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingCondition
member.summary: Class that represents a value comparison with the target of an arbitrary binding.

remarks.para: The class can compare a value a binding on any class within the current scope. Developers who want to compare to a value to a bound property on the control that contains the conditions surrounding may want to use a in addition to any binding conditions in the trigger list.

example.para: The XML example below creates a binding condition within a surrounding (omitted) that succeeds when the referenced name has a Text.Length property length that is equal to 0. (name must refer to an element, such as a , that has a Text property that, in turn, has a Length property.)
example.code: <BindingCondition Binding="{Binding Source={x:Reference name}, Path=Text.Length}" Value="0" />

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingCondition.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingCondition.Binding
member.summary: Gets or sets the binding against which the property will be compared.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindingCondition.Value
member.summary: The binding value that satisfies the condition.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Condition
member.summary: Base class for conditions.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DataTrigger
member.summary: Class that represents a condition on an arbitrary binding, along with a list of objects that will be applied when the condition is met.

remarks.para: The class is suitable for checking values on other controls, as well as any property on the control to which it has been added. That is, its default binding context is the control to which it has been added.

example.para: The XML example below prompts the user to type in the secret, which is, "The text color is green". When the user has typed "The", the Entry text changes color to red. When the user completes the secret, the Label text turns green. The code is turned back to the default color if the user then edits the secret. This example shows how to bind to the enclosing control (Entry from Entry), and to an arbitary control (Entry from Label)
example.code: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <ContentPage xmlns="http://xamarin.com/schemas/2014/forms" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml" xmlns:local="clr-namespace:TriggerDemo" x:Class="TriggerDemo.TriggerDemoPage"> <StackLayout VerticalOptions="Center"> <Label Text="Can you tell me the secret?" VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center"> <Label.Triggers> <DataTrigger TargetType="Label" Binding="{Binding Source={x:Reference checkEntry}, Path=Text}" Value="The text color is green"> <Setter Property="TextColor" Value="Color.Green" /> </DataTrigger> </Label.Triggers> </Label> <Entry Placeholder="Type the secret here." x:Name="checkEntry" VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center"> <Entry.Triggers> <DataTrigger TargetType="Entry" Binding="{Binding Text}" Value="The"> <Setter Property="TextColor" Value="Red" /> </DataTrigger> </Entry.Triggers> </Entry> </StackLayout> </ContentPage>

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DataTrigger.#ctor(System.Type)
member.param: The type of the object on which the data trigger will be added.
member.summary: Initializes a new instance.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DataTrigger.Binding
member.summary: Gets or sets the binding whose value will be compared to to determine when to invoke the setters.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DataTrigger.Setters
member.summary: Gets the list of objects that will be applied when the binding that is named by the property becomes equal to .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.DataTrigger.Value
member.summary: Gets or sets the value of the binding, named by the property, that will cause the setters to be applied.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.EventTrigger
member.summary: Class that represents a triggering event and a list of objects that will be invoked when the event is raised.

example.para: The example below shows how developers can use an event trigger to respond to events and update the value of a control property.
With the ColorTriggerAction class below defined in the TriggerDemo namespace:
the developer can use the XAML below to create a page that cycles the colors of a text area through the colors of the rainbow as the user enters text.
example.code: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <ContentPage xmlns="http://xamarin.com/schemas/2014/forms" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml" xmlns:local="clr-namespace:TriggerDemo" x:Class="TriggerDemo.TriggerDemoPage"> <StackLayout VerticalOptions="Center"> <Label Text="Do you see colors?" VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center" /> <Entry Placeholder="Type stuff here." VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center" BackgroundColor="Black"> <Entry.Triggers> <EventTrigger Event="TextChanged" > <local:ColorTriggerAction /> </EventTrigger> </Entry.Triggers> </Entry> </StackLayout> </ContentPage>

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.MultiTrigger
member.summary: Class that represents a list of property and binding conditions, and a list of setters that are applied when all of the conditions in the list are met.

remarks.para: Developers can use a to compare against property values on the control that contains it by using objects, or on any bound property (including those on the enclosing control) by using objects. These can be mixed in the same list.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.MultiTrigger.Setters
member.summary: Gets the list of objects that will be applied when the list of conditions in the property are all met.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PropertyCondition
member.summary: Class that represents a value comparison against a property on the control that contains the enclosing .

remarks.para: The class is suitable for checking property values on the control that contains the that contains it. To check values on other controls, developers can use the class, in addition to any property conditions in the trigger list.

example.para: The XML example below creates a property condition within a surrounding within a that turns the text green when the secret is typed. The code is turned back to the default color if the user then edits the secret. (Typically, a would have more than one condition; this example is simplified for clarity.)
example.code: <Entry Placeholder="Type the secret!" VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center"> <Entry.Triggers> <MultiTrigger TargetType="Entry" > <MultiTrigger.Conditions> <PropertyCondition Property="Text" Value="The text color is green" /> </MultiTrigger.Conditions> <Setter Property="TextColor" Value="Color.Green" /> </MultiTrigger> </Entry.Triggers> </Entry>

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PropertyCondition.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PropertyCondition.Property
member.summary: Gets or sets the property against which the property will be compared.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PropertyCondition.Value
member.summary: The binding value that satisfies the condition.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Trigger
member.summary: Class that represents a property condition and an action that is performed when the condition is met.

remarks.para: The class is suitable for checking the values of any property on the control to which it has been added. That is, its default binding context is the control to which it has been added. To bind on controls other than the parent, developers should use the class, instead.

example.para: The XML example below prompts the user to type in the secret, which is, "The text color is red". When the user has typed the secret, the Entry text changes color to red. The code is turned back to the default color if the user then edits the text to differ from the secret. This example shows how to bind to the enclosing control with a trigger.
example.code: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <ContentPage xmlns="http://xamarin.com/schemas/2014/forms" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml" xmlns:local="clr-namespace:TriggerDemo" x:Class="TriggerDemo.TriggerDemoPage"> <StackLayout VerticalOptions="Center"> <Label Text="Tell me the secret" VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center" /> <Entry Placeholder="Type the secret here." VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center"> <Entry.Triggers> <Trigger TargetType="Entry" Property="Text" Value="The text color is red"> <Setter Property="TextColor" Value="Red" /> </Trigger> </Entry.Triggers> </Entry> </StackLayout> </ContentPage>

example.para: The example below shows how developers can use a trigger to respond to events and update the value of a control property by using classes. The example prompts the user to answer a question about the color of the text, and then calls ColorTriggerAction to turn the text red when the user types "The text color is red". Developers should note that, while this example does not change the text back to the default color when the user continues to edit the string, the developer could additionally implement and specify an exit action to obtain that result.
With the ColorTriggerAction class below defined in the TriggerDemo namespace:
the developer can use the XAML below to create the page that responds to the secret string by changing the color of the text area to red.
example.code: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <ContentPage xmlns="http://xamarin.com/schemas/2014/forms" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml" xmlns:local="clr-namespace:TriggerDemo" x:Class="TriggerDemo.TriggerDemoPage"> <StackLayout VerticalOptions="Center"> <Label Text="What color is "The text"?" VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center" /> <Entry Placeholder="Type answer here." VerticalOptions="Center" HorizontalOptions="Center" BackgroundColor="White"> <Entry.Triggers> <Trigger TargetType="Entry" Property="Text" Value="The text is red" > <Trigger.EnterActions> <local:ColorTriggerAction /> </Trigger.EnterActions> </Trigger> </Entry.Triggers> </Entry> </StackLayout> </ContentPage>

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.IItemsLayout
member.summary: Defines the contract for an items layout that arranges items in collection and carousel views.
member.remarks: This interface serves as a marker for classes that define how items are laid out in views like and . Implementations include for single-row/column layouts and for grid-based layouts. The type converter attribute enables XAML parsing of layout specifications.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ItemSizingStrategy
member.summary: Specifies the strategy used to measure and size items in an items view.
member.remarks: The sizing strategy affects both performance and visual accuracy in collection views. For collections with uniform item sizes, use for better performance. For collections with varying item sizes, use for accurate layout.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ItemSizingStrategy.MeasureAllItems
member.summary: Each item is measured individually to determine its size. This provides the most accurate sizing but has a performance cost for large collections.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ItemSizingStrategy.MeasureFirstItem
member.summary: Only the first item is measured, and its size is used as a template for all items. This provides better performance but assumes all items have uniform size.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ItemsLayout
member.summary: Base class for layouts that arrange items in collection and carousel views.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ItemsUpdatingScrollMode.KeepItemsInView
member.summary: The view scrolls to keep currently visible items in view as items are updated. This is the default behavior, maintaining the current scroll position relative to visible items.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ItemsUpdatingScrollMode.KeepScrollOffset
member.summary: The view maintains the current scroll offset as items are updated. The absolute scroll position remains fixed, which may cause different items to become visible.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ItemsUpdatingScrollMode.KeepLastItemInView
member.summary: The view scrolls to keep the last item visible as items are updated. This is useful for chat-like interfaces where new items are added at the end.

member.remarks: When , items can be dragged and dropped between different groups during reordering operations. When , items can only be reordered within their own group. This property is only meaningful when the items view is grouped and is .

member.summary: Specifies the selection mode for selectable items views.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SelectionMode.None
member.summary: No items can be selected. Selection is disabled.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SelectionMode.Single
member.summary: Only one item can be selected at a time. Selecting a new item automatically deselects the previously selected item.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SelectionMode.Multiple
member.summary: Multiple items can be selected simultaneously. Users can select and deselect items independently.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsAlignment
member.summary: Specifies how items align to snap points in a scrollable items layout.
member.remarks: Snap points cause the scrolling to "snap" to specific positions, creating a paging-like effect. This enum controls where items align when they snap into position.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsAlignment.Start
member.summary: Items snap to align with the start of the scrollable area. For vertical scrolling, items align to the top. For horizontal scrolling, items align to the left (or right in RTL).

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsAlignment.Center
member.summary: Items snap to align with the center of the scrollable area. The snapped item appears centered in the visible viewport.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsAlignment.End
member.summary: Items snap to align with the end of the scrollable area. For vertical scrolling, items align to the bottom. For horizontal scrolling, items align to the right (or left in RTL).

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsType
member.summary: Specifies the snap points behavior when scrolling through items in an items layout.
member.remarks: Snap points control how scrolling behaves when the user stops interacting with the view. They create discrete stopping points, commonly used for paging or carousel-style navigation.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsType.None
member.summary: No snap points. Scrolling stops wherever the user releases, with standard inertia.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsType.Mandatory
member.summary: Scrolling snaps to the nearest snap point, but the user can scroll past multiple snap points in a single gesture. After scrolling stops, the view snaps to the nearest item.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SnapPointsType.MandatorySingle
member.summary: Scrolling snaps to exactly one adjacent snap point per gesture. The user can only advance or go back by one item at a time, regardless of scroll velocity. This is commonly used for to ensure precise item-by-item navigation.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Label.InvalidateMeasureIfLabelSizeable
member.summary: This method prevents unnecessary measure invalidations when the label is not sizeable. If the label has a fixed width and height, then no matter what the text is, the label will never change size.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Label.IsLabelSizeable(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Label)
member.summary: Determines if the label can grow in any direction based on the constraints. If the label cannot grow in any direction, then we usually don't need to do anything.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Label.TextChangedShouldInvalidateMeasure(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Label)
member.summary: Determines if the text has changed in a way that would require a measure invalidation. Unlike FormattedText changes, Text changes may not always require invalidation because the text size and spacing is all uniform. Formatted text may have a case where even though the label is a single line, the font size of a span may cause the label to grow vertically.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.AbsoluteLayout
member.summary: Positions child elements at absolute positions.
member.remarks: Application developers can control the placement of child elements by providing proportional coordinates, device coordinates, or a combination of both, depending on the values that are passed to method. When one of the proportional enumeration values is provided, the corresponding X, or Y arguments that range between 0.0 and 1.0 will always cause the child to be displayed completely on screen. That is, you do not need to subtract or add the height or width of a child in order to display it flush with the left, right, top, or bottom of the . For width, height, X, or Y values that are not specified proportionally, application developers use device-dependent units to locate and size the child element.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.AbsoluteLayout.AutoSize
member.summary: A value that indicates that the width or height of the child should be sized to that child's native size.
member.remarks: Application developers can set the width or height of the property to on a visual element when adding to the layout to cause that element to be measured during the layout pass and sized appropriately.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.FlexLayout.GetOrder(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindableObject)
member.param: The object for which to retrieve the property value.
member.summary: Returns the visual order of the element among its siblings.
member.returns: The visual order of the element among its siblings.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.FlexLayout.SetOrder(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.BindableObject,System.Int32)
member.param: The object for which to retrieve the property value.
The new flex order value.
member.summary: Sets the visual order priority of the element among its siblings.

remarks.para: Multiple elements may have the same ordering priority, in which case they will appear in the order in which they occur in the list. By default, the value of is 0.

For instance, if 3 children a, b, and c are added in order, and the developer wishes c to appear in the middle, the proper code is FlexLayout.SetOrder(b, 1). (A developer mistaking order priority for an index might wrongly expect FlexLayout.SetOrder(c, 1) to work, but as a and b will still have order priority 0, the elements in that case would still layout in the sequence a, b, c.

member.remarks: This property controls which edges of the layout should obey safe area insets. Use SafeAreaRegions.None for edge-to-edge content, SafeAreaRegions.All to obey all safe area insets, SafeAreaRegions.Container for content that flows under keyboard but stays out of bars/notch, or SafeAreaRegions.SoftInput for keyboard-aware behavior.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Page
member.summary: A that occupies the entire screen.
member.remarks: is primarily a base class for more useful derived types. Objects that are derived from the class are most prominently used as the top level UI element in .NET MAUI applications. In addition to their role as the main pages of applications, objects and their descendants can be used with navigation classes, such as or , among others, to provide rich user experiences that conform to the expected behaviors on each platform.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Page.DisplayActionSheetAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Maui.FlowDirection,System.String[])
member.summary: Displays a platform action sheet, allowing the application user to choose from several buttons.
member.param: Title of the displayed action sheet. Can be to hide the title.
Text to be displayed in the 'Cancel' button. Can be null to hide the cancel action.
Text to be displayed in the 'Destruct' button. Can be to hide the destructive option.
The flow direction to be used by the action sheet.
Text labels for additional buttons.
member.returns: A that displays an action sheet and returns the string caption of the button pressed by the user.
member.remarks: Developers should be aware that Windows line endings, CR-LF, only work on Windows systems, and are incompatible with iOS and Android. A particular consequence of this is that characters that appear after a CR-LF, (For example, in the title) may not be displayed on non-Windows platforms. Developers must use the correct line endings for each of the targeted systems.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Page.DisplayAlertAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Maui.FlowDirection)
member.summary: Displays an alert dialog to the application user with a single cancel button.
member.param: The title of the alert dialog. Can be to hide the title.
The body text of the alert dialog.
Text to be displayed on the 'Accept' button. Can be to hide this button.
Text to be displayed on the 'Cancel' button.
The flow direction to be used by the alert.
member.returns: A that contains the user's choice as a value. indicates that the user accepted the alert. indicates that the user cancelled the alert.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Page.DisplayPromptAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,Microsoft.Maui.Keyboard,System.String)
member.summary: Displays a prompt dialog to the application user with the intent to capture a single string value.
member.param: The title of the prompt dialog.
The body text of the prompt dialog.
Text to be displayed on the 'Accept' button.
Text to be displayed on the 'Cancel' button.
The placeholder text to display in the prompt. Can be when no placeholder is desired.
The maximum length of the user response.
The keyboard type to use for the user response.
A pre-defined response that will be displayed, and which can be edited by the user.
member.returns: A that displays a prompt display and returns the string value as entered by the user.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Page.OnDisappearing
member.summary: When overridden in a derived class, allows the application developer to customize behavior as the page disappears.
member.remarks: This method is called when the page disappears due to navigating away from the page within the app. It is not called when the app disappears due to an event external to the app (e.g. user navigates to the home screen or another app, a phone call is received, the device is locked, the device is turned off).

The current scale of the pinch gesture.
The updated origin of the pinch gesture.
member.summary: Constructs a new object with the specified values.

remarks.para: The origin of the pinch is the center of the pinch gesture, and changes if the user translates their pinch while they scale. Application developers may want to store the pinch origin when the gesture begins and use it for all scaling operations for that gesture.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UIModalPresentationStyle
member.summary: Enumerates valid modal presentation styles for iOS.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UIModalPresentationStyle.FullScreen
member.summary: The content is displayed in a manner that covers the screen.
member.remarks: The views belonging to the presenting view controller are removed after the presentation completes.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UIModalPresentationStyle.FormSheet
member.summary: The content is displayed in the center of the screen.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UIModalPresentationStyle.Automatic
member.summary: The system selects an appropriate presentation style for the view controller.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UIModalPresentationStyle.OverFullScreen
member.summary: The content is displayed in a manner that covers the screen.
member.remarks: The views belonging to the presenting view controller are not removed after the presentation completes.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UIModalPresentationStyle.PageSheet
member.summary: The content is displayed in a popover view.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UIModalPresentationStyle.Popover
member.summary: The content is displayed in a popover view.

member.summary: Indicates that no animation is preferred.

member.summary: Indicates that it is preferred that updates should slide in.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UpdateMode

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UpdateMode.Immediately
member.summary: Bound properties on picker elements should be continuously updated while the user scrolls.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific.UpdateMode.WhenFinished
member.summary: Bound properties on picker elements should be updated after scrolling is finished.

member.name: E:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PointerGestureRecognizer.PointerReleased
member.summary: Raised when the pointer that has previous initiated a press is released within the view.

remarks.para: Secondary / Right Button behavior (iOS & Mac Catalyst): When is set to include on iOS or Mac Catalyst, a secondary pointer press is simulated using an internal ("fake") context menu gesture – the same approach used by . Because UIKit does not expose a stable API for tracking a continuous right-button (secondary) down state, the framework will raise followed immediately by for a secondary click. There is no intermediate prolonged pressed state for secondary button interactions on these platforms.

If you need to distinguish a primary press/release sequence from a secondary one, inspect in the event handlers. Do not rely on timing (e.g., expecting a noticeable delay between pressed and released) for secondary interactions on iOS/Mac Catalyst as both events may fire in immediate succession.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PoppedToRootEventArgs

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ResolutionGroupNameAttribute
member.summary: Attribute that identifies a group name, typically a company name or reversed company URL, that provides a scope for effect names.

remarks.para: Developers must supply a name to that is unique over the scope of the name that they supply to . The method takes a string that is the concatenation of the name (the resolution group name), '.', and the unique name that was supplied to , and returns the specified effect.

example.para: For example, with the declarations:
example.code: [assembly: ResolutionGroupName ("com.YourCompany")] [assembly: ExportEffect (typeof (ShadowEffect), "ShadowEffect")]
example.para: Then the code below will add the effect to a button:
example.code: [var button = new Button { Text = "I have a shadow" }; button.Effects.Add (Effect.Resolve ("com.YourCompany.ShadowEffect"));

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ResolutionGroupNameAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.param: A name, such as a company name or reversed company URL, that helps to uniquely identify effects.
member.summary: Creates a new resolution group name attribute.

remarks.para: Developers must supply a name to that is unique over the scope of the that they supply to . The method takes a string that is the concatenation of (the resolution group name), '.', and the unique name that was supplied to , and returns the specified effect.

example.para: For example, with the declarations:
example.code: [assembly: ResolutionGroupName ("com.YourCompany")] [assembly: ExportEffect (typeof (ShadowEffect), "ShadowEffect")]
example.para: Then the code below will add the effect to a button:
example.code: [var button = new Button { Text = "I have a shadow" }; button.Effects.Add (Effect.Resolve ("com.YourCompany.ShadowEffect"));

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ScrollView.SafeAreaEdges
member.summary: Gets or sets the safe area edges to obey for this scroll view. The default value is SafeAreaEdges.Default (None - edge to edge).
member.remarks: This property controls which edges of the scroll view should obey safe area insets. Use SafeAreaEdges.None for edge-to-edge content, SafeAreaEdges.All to obey all safe area insets, SafeAreaEdges.Container for content that flows under keyboard but stays out of bars/notch, or SafeAreaEdges.SoftInput for keyboard-aware behavior.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SetterSpecificity
member.summary: Defines a setter specificity
member.remarks:
We still can refine the specificities, but here is how they're compared right now: - DefaultValue has the lowest priority - Everything coming from a Style is low priority - Binding, DynamicResource, Manual (in that order) - Values set from VSM have a higher priority
Then everything coming from the Handlers has a special priority. it is always applied, but is overridden by almost everything else

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.SetterSpecificity.#ctor(System.Byte,System.UInt16,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.UInt16,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Creates a new setter specificity
member.param:
Specifies special setter sources
- 1: from VSM
- 0xFF: from Handler

Determines manual specificity, also covers triggers
- 0: not manual
- 1..100: manual
- 101..N: triggers
Set to 1 when value comes from dynamic resource, otherwise 0
Set to 1 when value comes from binding, otherwise 0

XAML Style specificity
- 0: not from Style
- 127: base implicit style - 128-n: implicit style, n = max(99, distance between the RD and the target)
- 255: base local style - 256-n: local style, inline css,

CSS Id Specificity
See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Specificity

CSS Class Specificity
See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Specificity

CSS Type Specificity
See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Specificity

member.summary: Special private constructor to create DefaultValue specificity

remarks.para: For example, to create the style shown here: , developers could use either the following XAML or C#:

example.code: <ContentPage ... xmlns:controls="clr-namespace:Xaminals.Controls"> <Shell.SearchHandler> <controls:MonkeySearchHandler Placeholder="Enter search term" ShowsResults="true"> <controls:MonkeySearchHandler.ItemTemplate> <DataTemplate> <Grid Padding="10"> <Grid.ColumnDefinitions> <ColumnDefinition Width="0.15*" /> <ColumnDefinition Width="0.85*" /> </Grid.ColumnDefinitions> <Image Source="{Binding ImageUrl}" Aspect="AspectFill" HeightRequest="40" WidthRequest="40" /> <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Name}" FontAttributes="Bold" /> </Grid> </DataTemplate> </controls:MonkeySearchHandler.ItemTemplate> </controls:MonkeySearchHandler> </Shell.SearchHandler> ... </ContentPage>

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TabbedPage
member.summary: that displays an array of tabs across the top of the screen, each of which loads content onto the screen.

remarks.para: The user interface of a tabbed page consists of a list of tabs and a larger detail area. On iOS, the list of tabs appears at the bottom of the screen, and the detail area is above. On Android and Windows phones, the tabs appear across the top of the screen. The user can scroll the collection of tabs that are across the top of the screen if that collection is too large to fit on one screen.

App developers can create tabbed pages in either of two ways. First, application developers can assign a list of objects of a single class, or its subclasses, to the property and assign a to the property to return pages for objects of the least derived type. Second, app developers can add a succession of objects to the property. Both methods are shown in the code examples below.

example.para: The following example code shows how to display data of a specific type by assigning a to the property. Note how NamedColorPage gets its color by binding its field.
The example below creates a tabbed view with two instances.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TemplateBinding
member.summary: Binds a template property to the parent of the view that owns a .

remarks.para: Control templates provide reusable styling and functionality for common UI elements in an application. They do this by providing a common set of properties that bind to the parents of the View that owns the View to which the control template is bound.

example.para: Application developers can assign these properties through XAML, as in the following example:
example.code: <ControlTemplate x:Key="LoginTemplate"> <StackLayout VerticalOptions="CenterAndExpand" Spacing="20" Padding="20"> <Entry Text="{TemplateBinding Username}" Placeholder="Username" /> <Entry Text="{TemplateBinding Password}" Placeholder="Password" /> <Button Command="{TemplateBinding Command}" Text="Click Here To Log In" /> </StackLayout> </ControlTemplate>
example.para: Additionally, application developers can use the static method to set template bindings in code.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar
member.summary:
A control that provides title bar functionality for a window.

The title bar can also be hidden by setting the property, which will cause the window content to be displayed in the title bar region.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar.Icon
member.summary:
Gets or sets an optional icon image of the title bar. Icon images should be 16x16 pixels in size.
Setting this property to an empty value will hide the icon.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar.LeadingContent
member.summary:
Gets or sets a control that represents the leading content.
The leading content follows the optional and is aligned to the left or right of the title bar, depending on the . Views set here will be allocated as much space as they require.
Views set here will block all input to the title bar region and handle input directly.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar.Title
member.summary: Gets or sets the title text of the title bar. The title usually specifies the name of the application or indicates the purpose of the window

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar.Subtitle
member.summary: Gets or sets the subtitle text of the title bar. The subtitle usually specifies the secondary information about the application or window

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar.Content
member.summary:
Gets or sets a control that represents the content.
This content is centered in the title bar, and is allocated the remaining space between the leading and trailing content.
Views set here will block all input to the title bar region and handle input directly.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar.TrailingContent
member.summary:
Gets or sets a control that represents the trailing content.
The trailing content is aligned to the right or left of the title bar, depending on the . Views set here will be allocated as much space as they require.
Views set here will block all input to the title bar region and handle input directly.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.TitleBar.ForegroundColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreground color of the title bar. This color is used for the title and subtitle text.

member.summary: Gets the default template for the title bar

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ViewExtensions.InvalidateMeasure(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement)
member.summary: Layout updates can be forced by app code rather than relying on the built-in layout system behavior. However, that is not generally recommended. Calling InvalidateArrange, InvalidateMeasure or UpdateLayout is usually unnecessary and can cause poor performance if overused. In many situations where app code might be changing layout properties, the layout system will probably already be processing updates asynchronously. The layout system also has optimizations for dealing with cascades of layout changes through parent-child relationships, and forcing layout with app code can work against such optimizations. Nevertheless, it's possible that layout situations exist in more complicated scenarios where forcing layout is the best option for resolving a timing issue or other issue with layout. Just use it deliberately and sparingly.
member.param: The view on which this method operates.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.Height
member.summary: Gets the current rendered height of this element in device-independent units. This is a read-only bindable property.
member.value: The height of the element in device-independent units (DIUs).

remarks.para: The height of an element is set during layout.
Device-independent units (DIUs) provide a consistent unit of measurement across different screen densities. One device-independent unit equals one pixel on a 96-DPI display.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.HeightRequest
member.summary: Gets or sets the desired height override of this element in device-independent units. This is a bindable property.
member.value: The desired height in device-independent units (DIUs), or -1 if unset.

remarks.para: The default value is -1, which means the value is unset; the effective minimum height will be zero.
does not immediately change the of an element; setting the will change the resulting height of the element during the next layout pass.
Device-independent units (DIUs) provide a consistent unit of measurement across different screen densities. One device-independent unit equals one pixel on a 96-DPI display.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.InputTransparent
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether this element responds to hit testing during user interaction. This is a bindable property.

remarks.para: The default value is .
Setting to makes the element invisible to touch and pointer input. The input is passed to the first non-input-transparent element that is visually behind the input transparent element.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether this element is enabled in the user interface. This is a bindable property.

remarks.para: The default value is .
Elements that are not enabled will not receive focus or respond to input events.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.IsEnabledCore
member.summary: This value represents the cumulative IsEnabled value. All types that override this property need to also invoke the RefreshIsEnabledProperty() method if the value will change.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.InputTransparentCore
member.summary:
This value represents the cumulative InputTransparent value.
This method is not virtual as none of the derived types actually need to change the calculation. If this ever needs to change, then the InputTransparentContainerElement.OnCascadeInputTransparentPropertyChanged method should also call the RefreshPropertyValue() method - just like how the RefreshIsEnabledProperty() method does.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.IsFocused
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this element is focused currently. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: Applications may have multiple focuses depending on the implementation of the underlying platform. Menus and modals in particular may leave multiple items with focus.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.IsVisible
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that determines whether this element will be visible on screen and take up space in layouts. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: When an element has set to it will no longer take up space in layouts or be eligible to receive any kind of input event.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.MinimumHeightRequest
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum height the element will request during layout in device-independent units. This is a bindable property.
member.value: The minimum height in device-independent units (DIUs), or -1 if unset.

remarks.para: The default value is -1, which means the value is unset and a height will be determined automatically.
is used to ensure that the element has at least the specified height during layout.
Device-independent units (DIUs) provide a consistent unit of measurement across different screen densities. One device-independent unit equals one pixel on a 96-DPI display.

is used to ensure that the element has no more than the specified height during layout.

is used to ensure the element has no more than the specified width during layout.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.Opacity
member.summary: Gets or sets the opacity value applied to the element when it is rendered. The range of this value is 0 to 1; values outside this range will be set to the nearest valid value. This is a bindable property.

remarks.para: The default value is 1.0.

The opacity value has no effect unless is . The effective opacity of an element is the value of multiplied by the opacity of the element's Parent. If a parent has 0.5 opacity, and a child has 0.5 opacity, the child will render with an effective 0.25 opacity.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.Rotation
member.summary: Gets or sets the rotation (in degrees) about the Z-axis (affine rotation) when the element is rendered. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: Rotation is applied relative to and .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.RotationX
member.summary: Gets or sets the rotation (in degrees) about the X-axis (perspective rotation) when the element is rendered. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: Rotation is applied relative to and .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.RotationY
member.summary: Gets or sets the rotation (in degrees) about the Y-axis (perspective rotation) when the element is rendered. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: Rotation is applied relative to and .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.Scale
member.summary: Gets or sets the scale factor applied to the element. This is a bindable property.

remarks.para: Default value is 1.0.
is applied relative to and .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.ScaleX
member.summary: Gets or sets a scale value to apply to the X direction. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: The default value is 1.0.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.ScaleY
member.summary: Gets or sets a scale value to apply to the Y direction. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: The default value is 1.0.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.TranslationX
member.summary: Gets or sets the X translation delta of the element in device-independent units. This is a bindable property.
member.value: The X translation offset in device-independent units (DIUs).

remarks.para: Translation is applied post layout. It is particularly good for applying animations. Translating an element outside the bounds of its parent container may prevent inputs from working.
Device-independent units (DIUs) provide a consistent unit of measurement across different screen densities. One device-independent unit equals one pixel on a 96-DPI display.

member.value: The Y translation offset in device-independent units (DIUs).

remarks.para: Translation is applied post layout. It is particularly good for applying animations. Translating an element outside the bounds of its parent container may prevent inputs from working.
Device-independent units (DIUs) provide a consistent unit of measurement across different screen densities. One device-independent unit equals one pixel on a 96-DPI display.

does not immediately change the of an element; setting the will change the resulting width of the element during the next layout pass.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.Clip
member.summary: Specifies the clipping region for an element. This is a bindable property.
member.remarks: When an element is rendered, only the portion of the element that falls inside the clipping is displayed, while any content that extends outside the clipping region is clipped (that is, not displayed).

member.summary: Signals the start of a batch of changes to the elements properties. This can benefit performance if a bigger number of property values are changed.

When the operation is done, should be called.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.BatchCommit
member.summary: Signals the end of a batch of commands to the element and that those commands should now be committed.
member.remarks: This method only ensures that updates sent during the batch have been committed. It does not ensure that they were not committed before calling this.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualElement.Resources
member.summary: Gets or sets the local resource dictionary.

remarks.para: In XAML, resource dictionaries are filled with key/value pairs that are specified in XAML and consequently created at runtime. The keys in the resource dictionary are specified with the x:Key attribute of the XAML tag for the type to create. An object of that type is created, and is initialized with the property and field values that are specified either by additional attributes or by nested tags, both of which, when present, are simply string representations of the property or field names. The object is then inserted into the for the enclosing type at runtime.
Resource dictionaries and their associated XML provide the application developer with a convenient method to reuse code inside the XAML compile-time and runtime engines.
For more information, see: Resource Dictionaries (Microsoft Learn).

member.name: T:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.VisualState
member.summary: Contains a named visual state that applies a list of setters to an element in order to correlate a state with an appearance.

remarks.para: With visual state objects, developers can associate novel visual state names with a list of property setters that cause the control to represent that state. This frees the developer from relying on the set of provided states for a control, enabling finer control over the appearance of the application as the user's workflow progresses.
